cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The user is asking how the author introduces the topic of the article. The question is about identifying the method the author uses to introduce the topic. The options are A, B, C, or D. First, I'll read through the provided text to understand the structure. The article starts by asking several questions: "Do you start your work by making a list...? Do you always take a list...? Do you write down all the steps...?" These are all questions directed at the reader. Then, after the questions, the author states that if someone is a "natural list maker," this habit is a good thing and proceeds to list reasons why. The question is about how the author introduces the topic. The topic here is about the benefits of making to-do lists. So the introduction is the initial part before listing the reasons. The options are whether it's through questions, facts, the author's point of view, or listing benefits. Looking at the first part, the author uses several rhetorical questions to engage the reader and provoke thought. These questions are meant to make the reader consider if they are someone who makes lists, thereby leading into the discussion of the benefits. The next part of the text (the list of reasons) is the main body, not the introduction method. So the introduction is done by asking a series of questions.
